title: Connect Azure Services to a Cluster via Azure Service Connector

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Connector for Atlas has the following limitations:

- Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Connector for Atlas doesn't validate the connection string
  beyond checking for empty strings. Ensure your
  [connection string is valid](atlas-connect-to-deployment) to avoid
  runtime connection errors.

- Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Connector doesn't support all Azure (Microsoft Azure) regions. To learn more,
  see [Service Connector region support](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-connector/concept-region-support).

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
- An Azure (Microsoft Azure) account with an active subscription.

- An Azure (Microsoft Azure) compute service to connect to Atlas. To learn what Azure (Microsoft Azure) services
  the Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Connector supports, see the Azure (Microsoft Azure)
  [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-connector/overview#what-services-are-supported-by-service-connector).

- An existing Atlas cluster.

- A database user with the necessary permissions to access your Atlas cluster.

  > **Important:** Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Connector for Atlas
  > currently only supports [SCRAM](manual/core/security-scram) authentication.
  > To learn more, see [Configure Database Users](add-mongodb-users).

- Your Atlas cluster's connection string.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
Follow these steps to connect your Azure (Microsoft Azure) compute service (such as App Service)
to Atlas using the Azure (Microsoft Azure) portal.

sections.heading: Step 1: Navigate to your  compute service.
sections.body:
In the Azure (Microsoft Azure) portal, select the App Service, Function App,
or other compute resource you want to connect to Atlas.

sections.heading: Step 2: Open the Service Connector.
sections.body:
In the service's left-hand menu, scroll to the **Settings** section
and select **Service Connector**.

sections.heading: Step 4: Configure the connection.
sections.body:
Configure the following fields:

- **Service type**: Search for and select **MongoDB Atlas Cluster (preview)**.

- **Connection name**: Assign a descriptive name for this connection,
  for example, "atlas\_connection", or accept the auto-generated name. This
  name is for Azure (Microsoft Azure) management purposes.

- Click **Next: Authentication**

sections.heading: Step 5: Configure authentication.
sections.body:
The **Connection string** option is pre-selected as it's the only
supported method for Atlas.

- Enter your Atlas connection string. To find your connection string, see [Connect to MongoDB Search Community](manual/core/search-in-community/connect-to-search).

- (Optional) Edit the variable name that Azure Service Connector uses for
  your connection string. Click **Advanced** and click the pencil icon.
  Edit the variable name, then click **Done**.

- Click **Next: Networking**.

  Skip this step since you manage network access controls from your Atlas
  project settings, not through Service Connector's network configuration options.

  To learn about managing network access controls in Atlas, see [Configure Security Features for Clusters](setup-cluster-security).

- Click **Next: Review + Create**.

  Review the summary of the connection details. Verify the target service
  (Atlas), compute service, authentication method, and the environment
  variables that are created.

- Click **Create**.

  It might take a minute or two for Azure (Microsoft Azure) to provision the connection.
  Service Connector configures the necessary settings on your Azure (Microsoft Azure) compute
  service (primarily environment variables).

  After you create or modify an Azure (Microsoft Azure) Service Connector connection, the
  corresponding environment variables might not be immediately updated or
  reflected in your application's environment.

  Confirm the connection was created successfully by returning to the Service
  Connector page for your compute service. The new Atlas connection should
  be listed.

sections.heading: Using the Connection in Your Application
sections.body:
After you create the connection, Service Connector uses the connection details in
your Azure (Microsoft Azure) compute service's environment. Your application code accesses these
environment variables to connect to Atlas using a standard MongoDB driver or
library appropriate for your chosen language or framework.

sections.heading: Learn More
sections.body:
- [Overview of the Atlas/Azure Service Connector Integration](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-connector/how-to-integrate-mongodb-atlas?tabs=dotnet) in the Azure (Microsoft Azure) documentation.

- [Connect Your Atlas Cluster to an Azure App Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-connector/howto-mongodb-atlas-service-connection) in the Azure (Microsoft Azure) documentation.
